sourceMovie.title: Tomb Raider
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Tomb Raider for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Roar Uthaug keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Tomb Raider excels in action, Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest (2006) doubles down on adventure to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales (2017) is the natural next step after Tomb Raider: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and adventure elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Librarian: Quest for the Spear
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Tomb Raider who respond to its action will find The Librarian: Quest for the Spear (2004) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Librarian: Return to King Solomon's Mines
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Tomb Raider fans keep landing on The Librarian: Return to King Solomon's Mines (2006) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Uncharted
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Uncharted draws from the same action well as Tomb Raider, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2022 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Little Mermaid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Little Mermaid (2023) captures the adventure appeal of Tomb Raider without simply imitating it. The fantasy streak running through both films is what puts The Little Mermaid at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Peter Pan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The adventure appeal of Tomb Raider — that uplifting edge, the fantasy — translates cleanly into Peter Pan (2003). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Pete's Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Pete's Dragon (2016) clearly understood what makes adventure work — the same quality that keeps Tomb Raider rewatchable. Expect the same uplifting rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: King Kong
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: King Kong (1976) is the kind of film Tomb Raider fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The adventure overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Dante's Inferno: An Animated Epic
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dante's Inferno: An Animated Epic shares Tomb Raider's gift for action — the 2010 film delivers adventure without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Tomb Raider?
faq.answer: Try Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest, Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales, The Librarian: Quest for the Spear—each shares DNA with Tomb Raider while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tomb Raider?
faq.answer: Tomb Raider (2018) is best described as Action, Adventure, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tomb Raider worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
